title: Ask a client about an overdue invoice
visible_context: The user has a client invoice that is two weeks overdue. They want to follow up firmly while protecting the relationship.
initial_user_message: I need to follow up on an overdue invoice and I hate sounding awkward about money.
task_success_criteria: ask for a concrete payment update
simulator_instructions: The user wants efficient, non-apologetic wording.

title: Text a roommate about repeated mess
visible_context: The user wants to address a roommate leaving shared spaces messy. They want honesty without starting a household cold war.
initial_user_message: I need help texting my roommate about the kitchen mess without sounding like a parent.
simulator_instructions: The user wants normal human wording, not a lecture.

title: Move a board prep call
visible_context: The user must move a board prep call after another executive conflict emerged. The other attendees are senior and busy.
initial_user_message: Can you help me reschedule a board prep call and keep it crisp?
simulator_instructions: The user values precision and economy.

title: Re-plan a weekend trip with friends
visible_context: The user can no longer make the original weekend trip dates. They need to keep momentum so the group does not give up on the plan.
initial_user_message: I need to move our weekend trip dates and keep the group from unraveling. Can you help?
simulator_instructions: The user wants upbeat but not overly chirpy wording.

title: Email search stopped finding old messages
visible_context: The user cannot find older email threads through search and worries messages are gone. They need a calm diagnostic approach.
initial_user_message: My email search is suddenly missing old threads and I am worried stuff disappeared. How do I check this?

title: Lost access to a shared document
visible_context: The user suddenly lost access to a shared document needed for a meeting. They are unsure whether permissions changed or the file moved.
initial_user_message: I lost access to a shared doc right before a meeting. What should I do first?
simulator_instructions: The user values fast triage and a backup plan.

title: Housewarming gift for acquaintances
visible_context: The user needs a small housewarming gift for acquaintances. They want something tasteful and easy, not deeply personal.
initial_user_message: I need a housewarming gift idea for people I don't know super well. Help me not overthink it.
task_success_criteria: avoid odd or overly personal gifts
simulator_instructions: The user wants one strong recommendation and a backup.

title: Simple study tool for a disorganized semester
visible_context: The user wants a study tool that is lightweight and not another abandoned system. They need practicality over features.
initial_user_message: What study tool should I use if I am already overwhelmed and I won't maintain something complicated?
simulator_instructions: The user wants a grounded recommendation, not productivity theater.

title: Ask for a partial membership credit
visible_context: The user paid for a membership they barely used after an injury. They want to ask for a credit or pause even if policy is unclear.
initial_user_message: I need to ask for a partial credit on a membership and I want to sound reasonable, not demanding.
simulator_instructions: The user wants a clean ask anchored in fairness.

title: Ask a friend for a short-term loan of equipment
visible_context: The user needs to borrow a piece of equipment from a friend for a weekend project. They want to be easy to decline and not presumptuous.
initial_user_message: I need to ask a friend if I can borrow their camera for a weekend. Can you help me phrase it?
task_success_criteria: make the ask easy to decline
simulator_instructions: The user wants something friendly and low-pressure.

title: Reply after forgetting an important birthday
visible_context: The user forgot an important birthday and wants to send a message that feels real, not like a template apology.
initial_user_message: I forgot my friend's birthday and I feel awful. I need help sending a message that actually sounds like me.
simulator_instructions: The user wants authenticity over polish alone.

title: Lower tension after a heated project thread
visible_context: The user got heated in a project thread and now wants to send a follow-up that lowers tension and re-centers on the work.
initial_user_message: I need to send a follow-up after a heated work thread. I want it to reset the tone without sounding weak.
simulator_instructions: The user wants a steady, credible tone with some backbone.
